name: CASE 1: Mild Allergic Reaction (Discharge)
dischargeSummary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Patient presented with allergic reaction to peanuts.

CLINICAL FINDINGS:
Vitals on arrival: BP 110/70, Pulse 90 bpm, SpO2 99%.
Lab Insights: Unremarkable or pending..

DIAGNOSIS:
Diagnosed with Non-STEMI or Other.

TREATMENT GIVEN:
No critical medications administered.

HOSPITAL COURSE:
Patient was stabilized with appropriate medical management.

STATUS / DISPOSITION:
Patient is stable at discharge.

ADVICE / PLAN:
- Follow up with primary care in 3-5 days.
- Return to ER immediately if symptoms worsen.

name: CASE 2: Severe Trauma (Admit ICU)
dischargeSummary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Patient presented with rta, unconscious.

CLINICAL FINDINGS:
Vitals on arrival: BP 80/50, Pulse 130 bpm, SpO2 85%.
Lab Insights: Severe anemia, Possible shock.

DIAGNOSIS:
Diagnosed with Trauma.

TREATMENT GIVEN:
- IV Fluids / Blood: Titrate to BP
- Tranexamic Acid (TXA): 1 g over 10 mins

HOSPITAL COURSE:
Patient was stabilized with appropriate medical management.

STATUS / DISPOSITION:
Patient stabilized and transferred/admitted for ongoing care.

ADVICE / PLAN:
- Pending inpatient/ICU disposition orders.
- Family informed of condition.
